reviews.review_msg: Best prof ever. He is nice. You dont need the textbook if you always attend his class. His notes are awesome.
he is one of the best teachers i had so far. you cannot miss his class for sure. But he is no longer teaching at sfu, I miss him

In fact, I took his class in 2009 but I STILL CAN REMEMBER HIS LECTURE CONTENTS.  He was the most hot prof I've ever seen!!!!! Definitely take him as prof whoever wants to get good grade!!! TRUST ME AND GO FOR IT.
He's a humourous prof I ever seen! He keeps his class interesting all the time...however, the content of Econ 105 is kinda hard to me lol. Anyways. difintely recommend!!
I love this prof so much!! He is the best prof I've seen. Every Everything is so clear and so helpful. Hoping to take his class again

The best prof I've ever had at SFU. I've been looking for another class he's teaching, but I haven't found one yet. He is extremely approachable, profound in his course subject, and very humorous. Don't miss it, you won't regret.
Absolutely Amazing Prof. Hard Work Will payoff. Too bad hes in Edmonton now.
He is an awesome prof. Very helpful during his office hours. I recommend taking this course with him, but his tests are very tricky. I really liked his teaching style
His lecture note is too simple. Final supposes to be three hours, but he arranged it as two hours with double sized of mid-term questions. He is the teacher not so reponsable.
A good Professor I've never seen.

Tough exams, but Prof. Choi is helpful and cares about student success. Take good notes, use the free textbook, and try Unstuck AI for extra practice - it really helped me prep for the challenging quizzes.
Come to class and do your quizzes. Exams are so easy.  All the multiple choice questions are 100% copies and pasted from quizzes.
Took him for Econ 103 and 105. Not sure why people are saying he's a tough marker, when his test questions are all from his lecture notes. Go to class and pay attention, then you will do really well!

Jun's exams are tough as hell, ECON105 is not as hard as it looks like when you see his question paper. Except that, he is a good professor. He teaches good, he is caring, his grading system is also perfect and overall he is the teacher you should pick if you are ready to face his exams and quizzes offline

Jun is super nice and very willing to help and wants you to do well. He says first lecture if you have a problem with his accent then get a different prof, I got used to his accent very quickly and enjoyed the class although it was definitely a tough class
DO NOT TAKE THIS COURSE WITH HIM UNLESS YOU CAN LEARN ON YOUR OWN
Classes are boring, but the exam is straightforward

Basil seems like a cool guy but his lectures are hard to follow. If you intend on showing up to class sit close because he doesnt even speaks into his microphone. The textbook is very good and will be your best friend. TA is also very helpful but attendance was not mandatory. Exam included questions that were on none of the practice material or hw.

Professor Vasyl runs a relaxed yet engaging class. Tests are manageable with minimal studying. Showed his reviews and just laughed - what a legend! unstuckstudy was a great study aid alongside the textbook.
If satan or vasyl was teaching this class, take it with satan cuz tbh this class is literal hell

This guy is soooo old school. His lectures are done with a projector using a piece of paper and a pen. None of his notes make sense and you can barely understand what he says with his accent. Dont bother going to lectures. He doesnt post anything from lectures but you can learn everything from the textbook. Its a big snooze fest.

Horrible and unorganized lectures. All of your learning will come from the textbook. Picking up anything from lectures is extremely difficult. The way he teaches is incomprehensible, outdated, and confusing. Avoid his class at all costs.

Basil at first was really hard to understand and since I was a first year student, his lecture style intimidated me at first. After taking the first midterm however, his class became a lot easier since his tests are straight forward and relate to the material from the practice exams. TAs honestly make or break this class, get ready to read a lot.
I showed this man his reviews and he started laughing. GOAT.
the GOAT, this man is so relaxed and make learning economics worthwhile and interesting. Tests were also really chill and easy - minimal studying is required to get a decent grade.
No he did not in fact concoct a final from hell.
King Basil
Not organized
Great professor who is enthusiastic about the material. He explains concepts well and makes lectures interesting. Uses relatable real life examples well to illustrate course ideas.
Read the textbook, and you'll do well. He is a very fair grader and allows the final to replace any lower grades in the course. Exams were fair, and lectures helped with understanding.
She's a kind and approachable professor, but her exams are quite difficult, and the way she structures her questions can be confusing or unclear. Overall, I'd rate her 2.5 out of 5.

She is my favorite professor! I won't say her exams are easy, but if you look through the assignments and practice questions that she uploads, review the lecture materials, and ask her questions during office hours, you will be prepared!
The professor is great, willing to help my grade and give me a chance and is patient, if you study hard she will provide you with good marks.
I've never seen a professor write the wrong answers on exam solutions and then refuse to correct them

Jiamin is the worst econ professor I have encountered in my life. She is very tough grader. You can't even write the answers which are correct but not on her notes, so just try to gulp down everything she teaches. Also her papers are pretty tough and she tries to prove herself correct every time.
She made me stay one more term at FIC............
Oh my god! She gave me a first F in my study journey. I even passes Steve's 251 last term. I hate her!!!!
Guys, stay away from her. RUN!!!
Omg, so horrible.....
Too hard Instructor, come one, 100 level course!
I've never seen a professor like this. Year 1 class even harder than year 3, Jiamin, what are you doing?
If you wanna try the hardest exams that you've never seen, go to her class and you will see. Good luck in ECON105. Oh sorry, good luck in her classes.
The worst instructor I had in all terms at FIC...

I did find the lectures interesting but at times it was boring because the amount of content that was read straight from the PDF was majority and then explains the current state of the economy with real life examples. He forgives the midterm if the final is a higher score. The exams are easy if you understand the application of graphs and formulas.

Very knowledgeable prof. Lectures draw directly from textbook concepts and readings, with additional *spice* added by Professor Cox. Very easy tests -- if you are well prepared. To score highly, I suggest completing all assigned textbook readings before lecture to fully understand Professor Cox's analyses and explanations. Great guy!

He is a good prof. I think that he is a much better prof online rather than in person because his lectures move pretty slowly. Enjoyable to have on in the background at home but dry when in person. His lectures felt redundant and could have had more energy put into them.

I wouldn't be surprised if Yaser looked at his RMP because his past reviews don't correspond to how he teaches now. Yaser cares about his students and wants them to succeed--it's evident in how he's always available outside of class and makes sure we understand the content before moving on. I am running out of characters so in conclusion: gr8 guy!
Exams are nasty. Very nice and caring guy though.

Yaser is a good professor whose lectures combine real-life scenarios with the textbook content that he is teaching. He is very accessible outside lectures and will answer your questions with detail and passion. Few assignments and exams are appropriate in difficulty. Only problem is that tutorials videos are bad and TA marking is questionable.

Great Prof, clearly puts in a lot of effort and hopes all his students will succeed. Very approachable and available if any help is needed. The test are made up of questions extremely similar to the assignments and optional homework.
He is very helpful and really wants his students to be successful. The T.A.'s weren't really able to help but the professor was really good.

i actually really liked yaser and im glad i took this class with him. you can tell that he really wants his students to succeed. his lectures are pretty information heavy, so come to class prepared. he's very approachable and is always willing to help out his students. his exams were quite similar to the assignments and his previous exams.

Yaser was overall a great prof. He made his own lecture slides which summarized the textbook very well when supplemented with the lectures. Yaser was always available during his office hours and anytime through email (weekends too). It is evident that Yaser actually wants his students to succeed and proves this through his contact communication.

Very responsible and caring professor who will reply your emails even on weekends to answer your confusions regarding the assignments. The assignments and the previous exams are really good practice for the midterm. Doesn't necessary to read the textbook to do well in this course, do read the slides he posted on canvas.
Literally the worst prof ever. Puts in zero effort. Do not take this class with him

I took this course online, it was 100% asynchronous. You had to teach your self everything there was no help or guidance. He literally read out of the textbook and gave no further elaboration on course concepts. I would avoid this class if you can, he makes a hard class even harder.
Hard to understand what he is saying. Bad pronunciation

He is nice, although he doesn't speak good English. He is always willing to help and he is not like some other professors who like to give students ridiculous amount of assignments. His exam is related to the textbook and assignments. He also posts practice exam, which is very helpful.
Tony is a nice dude and he's course is very straight forward and simple. Easy class and nice prof. what more do you want?
Nice prof. I just suck at economics.

Tony is a fair prof. His midterms are easy if you sit down, read the textbook and keep up with the material. To manage this class it's important to consistently keep up. Overall, Tony was a great prof. Make good use of his office hours as he is always willing to help!
HORRIBLE accent and just reads off powerpoint slides
He is clear about what he expects from his students. Lectures are easy to understand and helpful while studying. GREAT PROF OVERALL!  Read the book, do mel practice, and keep up with the assignments!
The practice he provided was useful.
He doesn't care at all about students and gives out of syllabus exams . And nothing came from quizzes or study plan from mypearson in all midterms and final exams.

Vera is a nice prof definitely. Everyone got 100% for the online quizzes, only counts your highest score. Try it once, then refresh all your score before the final. If you refresh it too early, you can't make a good use of the study plan, which could be in the exam. She always pops some questions to let us think. Easiest Econ content in FIC.

Vera is very nice and sweet. I love how she always congratulates students on their exams and encourages them to work harder next time. Her lecture slides are not so organized, but it doesn't affect that much. Her quizzes are online and similar to the exams so if you work hard at home, it will pay off. Definitely a good prof!

Vera is the best economics instructor that I ever met!! She's patient, friendly and sweet. She can explain some obscure concepts in an interesting way. Besides, she always encourages students to keep up. I am so appreciative that I met such a nice instructor even though ECON is just my elective course. I can't get B+ without her!!
No Comments

OMG,don't choose this prof...he's the worst i've ever had,really..He doesn't teach at all,he just reads his book,he is always looking into his book and nothing more. The lecture is useless,every lecture all the studets fall asleep...so boring. He doesn't give any practice tasks. If you want to learn smth,do NOT choose him!

She's really nice, really passionate and loves to teach.  BUT, her exams and tutorial questions are so different than her course material.  They require a lot of interpretation and dissection that she doesn't directly teach you how to do, but expects you to be able to do.  I enjoyed the material, but it's hard to follow her in lectures.

Seriously, Brian made this class easy, he gave us past tests and practically copy and pasted those same questions on the midterms and finals (no surprises). His lectures were difficult to sit through, but the online recordings were even harder to listen to, go to lecture its not even that bad and it WILL help you grade. Homework is easy marks DO IT

Course structure was superb, everything crystal clear. Past and sample exams were gold. I would not say his lectures were awesome, but they were quite good. Very good at answering your questions. His excellent organization of the course made it really digestible and even easy at times. Kinda a lot of info tho so just be sure to keep up.
He gives you past exams that are almost exactly like the tests. As long as you practice those and understand key concepts you're good. He's also pretty funny.

Lectures are quite heavy but he gives you practice exams that are quite similar to the actual exams. Study the practice exams and you will do great. The lectures are heavy and he goes through nearly 100 slides and assigns weekly online quiz questions to do. Fair class and would take again

I love the way Brian ran ECON 105. Attendance is optional, but tutorials are key since tutorials problems are very similar to exam questions and the answers are only available in tutorial. He provided us with a practice exam, with a key, for each exam. His exams were completely fair and reflected the tutorial problems and homework.

Great prof. Great lectures and course layout. Brian clearly cares about his students. He provides all the resources necessary for you to succeed. You have to buy the textbook due to weekly quizzes linked to it but don't bother reading it. Lectures, tutorials and studying are enough. The content taught is valuable for everyday life. Highly recommend

Nice guy who wanted the students who tried in the class to succeed. If you are able to do the homework that he gives out the tests are essentially the same thing. He gives tons of recourses to help you prepare so the midterms and finals have no surprises.

Brian was a pretty chill prof. Attendance wasn't mandatory. The textbook was through Achieve Learning and there were weekly assignments that took 40mins max to complete, so it was pretty good. The exams are very similar to the practice exams he gives. Overall, if you understand the basics, you're gonna do fine.
Great prof who genuinely cares about his students and stays up all night for office hours to help you succeed. Also is extremely transparent so you know exactly where you stand, always,
Avoid this professor for ECON 105 please! The exams are so hard, if you don't know the current news, you won't be able to answer the questions!
Absolute god.
Don't really understand what he was saying, but his powerpoints are great. Do the homework he assigned because exam will be based off homework.
Poor english skills, hard to understand. Due to the lack of communication he doesn't explain very well. Midterm questions are much like those given in class
he's average

Lectures are quite dry and he took out majority of the math. Wastes time in lecture answering pointless questions with little relevance to econ. Explanations were quite lacking or convoluted. Added in lectures that were not originally in the syllabus. HW marking standards were weird. Exams were ok but he could forget to mark yours. Nice guy tho.
Funny guy, engages with students. Explained concepts well, if a little bit slowly. Tests and final were fair. All around great prof.

Eliav can be unorganized sometimes, but he is very kind. TA's for this class can be unhelpful sometimes, but I noticed the course was based around his lectures so quizzes we're easy, practical and fair. Midterm and final wasn't horrible just make sure you review.

Macroeconomics was not an easy course, however, it was not difficult to achieve a high grade in this class. he gave us problem sets that are easy to get 100% on if you try, because they are not marked for correct answers but for completion. We also had quizzes that were a little bit tough but doable and there were no surprises on his exams.

Currently taking Econ 105 with him and he is a great professor. He has been really helpful and really wants his students to do well. Lectures are a bit boring, but only because most 3 hour lectures are boring in general, so you can't blame him. Glad I took his class

He is good but he is very disorganized and his exams do not cover everything from lectures so some questions are confusing. However, he gives good participation marks and show your work marks which I like and he seems nice. However, he can be intimidating to approach.

He seems to have changed the material he teaches so it has become so much easier. He has a good sense of humour and makes lectures go fast and even let people go sooner when he finishes the lecture one hour early. The course has similar things to Econ 103 so if you liked it, take this course.

Doesn't use Canvas for grades, so you're completely lost for that. Gets distracted in lectures, quizzes are okay, but exams are insane. One of his TA's is impatient and rude. His final is accumulative, so you have to remember formulas from his first lecture. Dude is completely disorganized, do not take his class.

This was one of the most disorganized classes I've ever taken. Eliav doesn't even use canvas, so I'll have no idea where I stand in this CURVED class until I get my final mark. Additionally, he doesn't even have a suggested textbook so I couldn't pre-read before class without searching through a random Macroeconomics textbook to find the topics.

Harris is the most boring prof you will ever have. He is unenthoused and monotone. His exams are extremely easy though. The midterm for econ 105 was all multiple choice and some of the questions were so easy they made me laugh. You'll have to teach yourself mainly from the textbook, but you'll probably get a decent mark at least.
he can be boring, but i found him decend enough to get me interested in the course. exams are fully based on textbook, good prof :)

Clearly knows his material, although most of what's explained in lecture is never tested. Exams and midterms are stolen off of textbook website, and makes very little effort to answer any emails or clarify things. Boring as hell, lectures are not actually mandatory unless you want a 10% clicker grade.

I don't know why ppl say he is a hard prof. If you only understand his notes and do all the quetions he provides for every tutorial, I bet you can get a b+ at least. I am a non-english speaking student and took his course for my first term, finally I got A+ and my friend who is also a non-english speaking guy got an A. So, I think the locals should

Such a hard class!  Aside from the many issues I had with my TA, Les was a bit of a nightmare.  In order to cram way too much info into lecture, we gives "notes"; but for those of us who need to write notes to learn, he has NO sympathy.  I had to sacrifice my social life in order to scrape by with a "B" in this class.  Don't take this class!
organized, good notes, not bad at explaining.....exam questions were very fair, might be a little too long tho, gotta know the material well to finish it on time

Listen in class and take notes! Lecs and tuts are a must. MT material was easy. Just make sure you understand the main concept and ask for help if needed. TA had a heavy accent but it was okay. Got an A on MT and ended up with A-. I thought the final commentary questions were hard. Doing the hwk is key! Overall,not bad.

Mr. Marshall is a professor who's a genius at what he KNOWS. However, it doesn't make him one when he cannot explain in the easiest sense what he's talking about. The terminology in itself is really hard to understand. He further complicates this task when he's unable to provide clear examples. Also, his TA's don't know anything about Economics.

this prof is just rediculous with his damn TAs. Questions in MT and finals are all short or LONG answers. no freakin Mc at all. each question worth at least 5-20marks. so good luck guys if you gonna take this course with him. im on probation just cuz of this damn prof

yeah, crazy marking scheme. If you think you are good at economy, don't take it with this prof, otherwise you will be surprised when seeing your final grade. I didn't take the comments here, and i took this course. I knew all the concepts, and i was hoping to get A+, but i got A-. Avoid him -- comment from student with CGPA over 4.0

course is easy, but their marking is really stupid. You have to write all the garbage info to get over 80%. The TA called IDEEN is really****(not sure about the others). He can't speak English, and doesn't know economy at all. If you copy his answer, sure you won't get 100%. Avoid this prof if you can to avoid the unfair marking -- seriously

He has good notes, although you have to attend lecture to fill some stuff. His exams are straight from his notes but the marking is way too hard. Amount of detail needed is ridiculous. May waste too much time talking about unnecessary things and don't bother asking him questions. he'll brush you off or wont listen to you entirely.

Of the worst classes I have had at Alexander College, Professor Junjie Liu only reads the slides, when grading only see the results and does not observe the procedures, makes evaluations with more than 20 formulas that must be memorized. His accent is very complicated to understand, for a course of this level the professors should have good diction

I took Junjie's Econ 105 at FIC. His lecture slides are more than enough for you to do well in his class. He gives lots of practice questions. His exam questions are very similar to the practice questions.
Nice prof, easy exams.
He made me love Econ, definitely recommend!!!

The class only had two midterms and a final. His lectures included him looking at the screen and turning around and reading what was on the slides to the class. He did barely any practice problems in class so going to lecture seems like a waste of time. was not very accessible either. Best hope is to do the practice problems. Tests were mostly fair
Good teacher, sometimes hard to understand. Exams are extremely difficult and content oriented, but the class is curved, which balances the grades.

Yeah he has a bit of an accent but you can overcome this! He is extremely helpful if you ask him questions!! He wants his class to be very involved. However, his lectures are super boring! Tests are straight from the book and lecture notes (lecture notes are literally based from the book)! Pay attention and practise the problems and youll do fine!
He cares about the students too much. Lectures are very informative and are clear. Assignments are well related. Exams are fair. He is pretty good.
He's pretty good

Martin is a very well organized and coordinated professor. I am not an econ major but I really enjoyed his class by far the best prof I've ever had. Just summerise the powerpoints and read those. Also do the practice queestions. I literally put very little effort and still got an A.

I love his class, even though his lectures were 3 hours long he would always them make them somewhat bearable with his dry witted jokes. Hes a funny guy and his practice exams are very similar to his actual ones. Grind the practice exams if you want to do well. The only downside of this course was that your entire grade was based on 3 exams.

Since it's a 3 hour lecture, you have to make sure you are focused. Prof Santamaria is really funny and he tries to make the lectures digestable which is nice. I think they recently changed how the course is because because everything pre summer 2023 practice exams is unreliable. other than that, 2nd and 3rd exam was not that bad!
Tends to ramble sometimes but overall great prof. If you study the slides and do his practise questions you'll be fine!

Great proof, minimal work load if you understand concepts only 3 tests, no assignment, projects etc. Perfect course if you are somewhat okay at exams and lazy/ don't want to do projects & assignments.

Good prof, exams are exactly like the ones on the practice exams so make sure to do them. He does 3 tests (not cumulative) and has no final so ez class. lectures very long up to 3 hours and can be boring but he cracks some good jokes. lots of work so be on top of ur homework and be passionate about econ and youll do good.
The professor makes the class easy - practice exams are similar to the real ones. Using Unstuck study alongside the provided resources can really help with exam prep.
Ez class, practice exams are the same as the real ones.

He's a great guy. He sometimes adds a few jokes in his lectures. The 3 hour lectures could be a lot because of how content-heavy this course is. There was also a lot at stake with your entire grade being determined by only 3 exams. Otherwise, he is a good lecturer and explains the concepts pretty well. Just go to lectures and tutorials.

out of all the lower level econ teachers he is the best. lectures were ok (not boring but not super interesting) - maybe because it was 3 hrs. tests were relatively fair and make sure you look at the sample tests. questions are mostly marked 5-10 so if you mess up it can cost you a lot. way easier than econ 105 but you have to remember more.

Took Econ 105 and 355w with him. Very straightforward. His lecture slides and previous midterm/finals will help you a lot. Make sure you practice those and he does give hints if you attend lecture. Nicest dude. Tries to make jokes and it is even funnier when no one laughs!

His exams are incredibly fair, very similar to practice questions. His past exams are your best friend as his tests are heavily based off of them. This class is not hard if you remotely put effort into it. He is a fair and great teacher.

Chapter presentation documents are disorganized, jumping from topic to topic, and then back again. All materials are littered with grammatical mistakes, including exams! Lectures are hard to follow and boring. His accent is hard to understand, and he does not speak slowly. He will go on tangents that start interesting but leave you more confused.

He is the easiest, most relaxed professor in all of ECON 105. All the exams are individual and not cumulative (all the questions are basically similar to the exams examples he gives), highly recommended!

Don't look at the other reviews about students complaining about this teacher. He does a great job teaching the content and all the lectures are recorded. All of his exams are incredibly fair, they are either complete copy and paste from past exams or they are straight from the textbook questions he assigns. If you at least try, its not that hard.
Exam 1 was so easy, Exam 2 was a hell of a grind, too hard, too scary. Especially a lot of questions about the class example, even if you go to listen to the class will not do.

Martin's lectures are difficult to follow and stay engaged in, as he strictly lectures the entire 3 hours. He formats the order of the chapters poorly, making the course very disorganized. Despite the syllabus stating that the exams are not cumulative, he put questions from previous chapters that were already tested on the second midterm.

I barley went to any lectures and mostly just self-learned from the slides however when i did go i loved his lectures, they were very engaging. He has three midterm type tests and no final. Each midterm is based on different chapters. Since this is a content heavy class, i loved that he didn't have a cumulative final at the end. Tests were easy.

Martin is amazing! He talks a lot but it's all good vibes. I never showed up to class..maybe like three times.. but whenever I did, it was always a blast! Take ECON105 with Martin, my Argentinian king

Very interesting and worthwhile class and prof. Quizzes and exams are challenging, but are doable by explaining thoughts of material from class. TA is helpful. Marks scaled up later. Sit back for half the lecture about random stuff with econ remarks, picking on students, or silly jokes. Being a well paid goalie beats 5 bucks a day plus food coupons

Biggest problem with Yan Yan was that he wasn't much help during office hours. It's a really hard class but he just told me to do my best so he's supportive at least. Also he curves everything so it evens out somewhat. Tends to talk in circles sometimes and is a little disorganized, but it was never a huge problem. Go to ur TA instead for help
YAN YAN IS THE GOAT
Amazing professor
Great prof recommend everyone to take his classes if available. Sit in the front of class so it's easier to hear him out during lectures.

Yan Yan is the GOAT! He's the professor I've ever had, he's funny, his lectures are engaging, and he CURVES GREAT! He'll explain/clarify concepts for you, change the weightage of exams if you show improvement, and his exams are fair. Do practice problems (especially long answer) and study sets every week, without fail, and you'll be perfectly fine!

took him for 103, 105 felt much harder material wise. tests focus heavily on application that is based on lots of theory, but his exams are fair if you prioritize doing long-answer questions.

prof is very willing to help you understand topics, goes through lectures at a good pace. his difficulty is fair, but your study habits are important.

Yan is my favorite prof I've had at SFU. 
He is funny and the lectures are engaging, his accent doesn't change the fact he makes ECON easy to understand unlike most profs. If you just read the textbook and do the practice problems you'll be more than ready for the exams. I highly recommend Yan and hope to take classes with him again. e

Yan Yan is by far one of my favorite professors at SFU. Very caring, kind, and helpful outside of class. Midterm and final is not hard if you study the lectures and do the problem sets. His accent is understandable and he's overall just a great professor, would take again 100%. Very funny too lol.

ECON 105 felt a lot harder than 103 tbh, way more abstract and packed with concepts. I did think about dropping at one point. But Yan made the hard stuff feel manageable. He's super encouraging (like actually), which helped way more than I expected. Glad I stuck with it.

Good professor, provided us with problem sets which gave a good insight into what was going to be on the exams. Attending lectures are very optional, there are pop quizzes however. His accent makes it very hard to learn and understand what he's saying.

My favourite professor at SFU so far. He is funny, considerate, and kind but still challenging. His lectures are genuinely helpful and engaging, and gets me excited to learn. Study properly and you will do well. There are participation mark "pop" quizzes worth 20% which grant free 5/10 pts as long as you submit anything at all.

I didn't take any econ in high school, so it's more technical than I thought it'd be. But Yan's really good at breaking it down and making it make sense for us. You can tell he actually cares about his students. It'd be even better if there weren't any quizzes though!
Awesome prof! Even during the boring parts, like calculating GDP, CPI and all that stuff, he knows how to vibe with us and keep things chill.
It's really amazing time to study this course with Prof.Yan!
He is so nice, with clear examples and easy exams! Highly recommend at all!!!
Never again.

-Read of slides every lecture that he posted (doesn't really teach)
-the average for my midterm was 40%
-If you didn't take any macro prior to this course it's rough
just don't. Just no...

Will take points off of an exam if you add too much/feels it is irrelevant. Admits his tests are too/sometimes can't even solve his own questions. Has VERY low averages for tests so he has to curve very high to be on par with other 105 classes. READ EVERYTHING on the slides,

Respectfully, take this course with another teacher. John has no clue how to teach. If you have no choice but to take this course, read the textbook like it's the Bible; consistently and religiously. That textbook is the only thing saving me. Also, the final is 50% of the grade, which I'm taking in less than an hour. Pray.

The course material itself is not to difficult, however I found the way he taught concepts to be confusing. It felt sort of like the class was taught out of order. If you take this course make sure you study concepts on your own outside of his lectures. His midterm was fair but difficult as understanding the concepts is not enough to do well.

I am at a loss for words, this man needs to be stopped. John Knowles can't keep getting away with this. If you see this rating, never take Econ 105 with this man. Run. Run far far away until you have reached a safe place. From his lectures to his tutorial quizzes to midterm and now the final, it has been a horrific,awful,disintegrating experience.

Don't let any other classes fool you. John Knowles 105 class is a nightmare. The lecture slides are no help and the man refuses to use a microphone but its okay he barley talks about the class topic. I learned more economics in high school. But the last straw was when he kept getting his own practice final wrong. Shame on SFU for letting him teach.

By far the least competent professor at the entirety of SFU. There is no point in going to his lectures as he reads everything he teaches off of the slides or from the textbook verbatim. He often doesn't know how to answer the questions he poses and literally provides nothing of substance, I stopped attending and found my grade improved.

There are professors that want to see you do well and then there profs like Knowels. There's no cohesion to his ECON 105 course, it feels like a bunch of random topics thrown together at random. To add to it, he cannot explain those topics very well. His classes are also indescribably boring--every single one.
cheeks. dont take

Lectures were based off his slides. He did NOT know his own material and often backtracked on everything he said. Mind numbingly boring and a terrible teacher overall. One time a question popped up on the slides and he said "I actually don't know how to solve this, but it'll probably be on the mid-term". He overcomplicates easy concepts.

Stubborn and fixated on extremely dated takes on teaching, he ambles through his lectures almost completely incompetently. His first comment to the class was essentially "Professors arent supposed to be good at teaching, high school teachers are better at that". Overall, extremely unserious about his job and difficult in his ways. Not recommended!

Lectures were almost purely off of his poorly made slides, point by point. Barely knew his own material and often time had to stop himself to figure out what he was supposed to be saying. Tutorial quizzes were largely a guessing game in regards to the material covered. Gave review material without answers or weeks late at best.Testing was not clear
Funny Turkish guy. Cant understand lectures. Dont take with him unless you like reading.

He sounds like French Gru. Nice dude but goes off on tangents; an immense amount of outside effort and work is required to succeed in this course, due to his digressions from course content in lectures. We had to skip 3-5 chapters because he was going through content slowly, and/or distracted with material not required in this course
Not sure why he even is a professor. It's harder to be that bad of a teacher than to be a good one. He had to be trying to do a bad job because that quality of teaching was unbelievable.

Gorkem is a wonderful person, nice guy and always available to answer questions or talk.

Unfortunately, he is not a very good professor. Lectures ramble, meander and are confusing, so be prepared to do a lot of self-study involving the textbook. He's also very poor at keeping to schedule, we skipped 4 chapters cause we ran out of time.

My initial plan was to take econ as a minor, but after this course, I am very confident that I never even want to hear the word economy ever again in my life. I read the whole textbook twice, attended every lecture and tutorial, and did every bit of take-home work that was provided and I still managed to fail quiz after quiz and the midterm

Gorkem is a very sweet guy that very much cares for his students. Midterm and Final were very fair and straight from the Textbook. However, he is not that good at lecturing, so be prepared to read and study yourself. However, he is very sweet in office hours.

You need to put in the time to do good in this class. His lectures aren't too engaging but they help you understand a lot when reading through the textbook. Midterm and final questions were either straight from the textbook or involved a little bit of critical thinking (easy if you read the textbook). Final also had 2 questions straight from mt.
Quizzes are hard but fair. Makes you think more conceptually on the topics. Easy marker and he made the final worth less if you do poorly on it
As far as econ lectures go Gorkem is pretty average. However, he cares about his students and adjusts midterms and quizzes to make sure they are the right difficulty.

Do not take this guy. He is very nice and caring but is simply way too unorganized. In my opinion, I have no idea how he has unlimited supplies of water he drinks during lecture. Also his mic is always broken. Quizzes are next level and refuses to give answers to the textbook.
